observations.observation: A new brand labeled "Store" appeared in the top brand list this snapshot — an ambiguous merchant name that likely represents a store-brand or unresolved merchant. This is the second consecutive heartbeat with a new brand entry (Starbucks appeared last cycle), suggesting the merchant diversity is expanding but resolution quality is inconsistent.

Token velocity recovered slightly to 0.55x with acceleration at 1.037x — a mild positive after the sharp 0.531x deceleration last heartbeat. The 7d trailing window ticked up from $24,539 to $24,716 (425→434 receipts), suggesting the rolling-window artifact that caused the sharp drop has partially cleared. Recovery is modest, not a reversal.

3 new CBSAs appeared this snapshot (non-metro MA, CBSA 29420, non-metro AZ), bringing total active metros to 17. The burst-quiet-burst pattern continues: after the large 31-CBSA peak, the network has been expanding in smaller increments (3 new this cycle), with geographic diffusion rate at 17.6% of active CBSAs.

All 5 open vendor requests on the frontier are SaaS/infrastructure vendors (Notion, Render, Vercel, DigitalOcean, Hetzner) — a distinct category from the current physical retail dataset. If approved, these would represent the first digital-native spend tokens in the pipeline, potentially expanding the cross-domain signal beyond its current 0.7% ratio.

Starbucks appeared in the top brand list for the first time this snapshot, joining King Soopers and other established brands. This is a new category signal — a national QSR/coffee chain entering the verified spend dataset alongside grocery and home improvement brands.

Token velocity dropped sharply to 0.531x (from 0.639x last heartbeat) with acceleration at 0.832x — the steepest single-heartbeat deceleration in recent cycles. The 7d trailing window fell from 525 to 425 receipts ($29,587→$24,539), a 100-receipt drop that likely reflects a rolling-window artifact as a strong intake day aged out of the 7d window.

Token velocity ticked up to 0.639x (from 0.626x last cycle) with acceleration at 1.021x — the first meaningful velocity increase after 3 consecutive flat heartbeats at ~0.624x. Geographic expansion (4 new CBSAs) and velocity recovery are occurring simultaneously, suggesting the two signals are coupled: geo bursts precede or coincide with velocity inflections.

Total receipts crossed 4,013 this snapshot with cumulative GMV at $233,844 — the 7d trailing window jumped from $29,224 to $29,587 (513→525 receipts), a 12-receipt gain in 3 hours suggesting intake is accelerating again after the post-burst consolidation.

4 new CBSAs appeared this snapshot (17410, 37980, 16700, 19820), pushing total active metros to 31 — the network has now crossed 30 active CBSAs for the first time. This is the third consecutive burst phase: 13-CBSA burst → 3-heartbeat quiet → 8-CBSA burst → 3-CBSA burst → now 4-CBSA burst, with the network expanding in waves rather than linear growth.

Token velocity at 0.626x with acceleration exactly 1.000x — the system is in near-perfect steady state. After the post-burst consolidation (3 heartbeats at ~0.624x) and the new geo burst resuming, velocity is flat but geographic diffusion is active again, suggesting intake growth is happening at the edges rather than the center.
